expectations.text: States that implementation uses the tdd skill with an explicit red-green-refactor loop.
expectations.evidence: Phase 5 quotes the skill directly: 'Always use the tdd skill to implement with an explicit red-green-refactor loop. Treat this as required for Implementation on every track; do not bypass it.' Then enumerates the loop: Red (write failing tests), Green (implement minimal code), Refactor (clean up without breaking tests).

expectations.text: States that code-simplifier is launched as a subagent for refactoring opportunities.
expectations.evidence: Rule 1 in Phase 6 states: 'Always launch an independent code-simplifier subagent to identify refactoring opportunities after implementation.' Subagent role is named as 'code-simplifier', sourced from '../code-simplifier/SKILL.md', and its purpose is described as independently reviewing for redundancy, over-engineering, naming quality, and structural clarity. Also appears in the final Exact Subagent Roles Summary table.

expectations.text: States that quality review is performed by an independent code-reviewer subagent.
expectations.evidence: Rule 2 in Phase 6 quotes: 'Always run independent code-reviewer agents for quality review on every track.' Names three parallel code-reviewer agents with distinct focuses and notes they operate independently without shared state.

expectations.text: Keeps recommendations calibrated to medium-sized feature work instead of escalating unnecessarily.
expectations.evidence: Track Selected is Standard with explicit rationale: 'not so large or unclear that Deep is warranted.' Standard-track reviewer scaling (multiple code-reviewers) is applied without escalating to Deep-track extras. Response does not add unnecessary ceremony.
